description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, jagged surface and a mix of blue-gray and off-white coloration. The rock displays a crystalline texture, with some areas appearing more granular and others smoother, likely due to natural weathering. There are visible patches of lighter material, possibly mineral deposits or weathered surfaces, contrasting with the darker, denser stone. On one of the flatter faces, there is a faint inscription or marking, suggesting prior human handling or cataloging. The rock's overall form is angular, with several sharp edges and a few flatter planes, making it appear as though it was broken from a larger formation. Its size and mass would make it a significant obstacle if placed in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside.
description: A jagged, blue-gray and off-white rock with rough, crystalline texture and visible markings on one face. Its angular form and substantial size make it a prominent obstacle or natural feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, highlighting its blue-gray crystalline texture and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a flatter, lighter-colored surface with visible human-made markings or inscriptions.
description_view_2: Here, the rock's elongated shape and a mix of rough and smoother areas are visible, with more pronounced mineral color variation.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the narrowest end, showing a pointed, angular tip and a blend of blue-gray and off-white tones.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside area
